_comment: Pilot batch — ~6 representative assets across all four locked categories plus the risky 4-frame sprite-sheet attempt. Generate, review quality + actual API cost, THEN scale to the full bank. Output paths anchored to the consuming code (factories) and ADR-0022 audio paths. NOTE: assets/art/classes/ and assets/art/enemies/ are TRACKED ship art (the idle sprites + portraits + enemy sprites are already committed — PRs #216-218; .gitignore only ignores assets/art/demo/ + assets/audio/demo/), so generated art committed to those paths needs NO .gitignore change — it is review-only until artistic sign-off, then committed as ship art with its .import sidecars.

sfx.prompt: A soft, warm, cozy UI confirm tap — a single gentle wooden-and-felt click with a faint lantern-warm chime tail. Clean, short, satisfying, low-key. No harsh transient, no reverb wash.

music.prompt: A warm, cozy fantasy guild-hall ambient music bed. Gentle fingerpicked acoustic guitar and soft hammered dulcimer over a low hearth-fire warmth, relaxed and unhurried, seamlessly loopable, no percussion build, no vocals. Evokes a lantern-lit medieval hall at dusk where adventurers rest between dungeon runs.

backgrounds.prompt: Dungeon-biome backdrop diorama: 'Forest Reach' — an overgrown ancient woodland dungeon approach. Mossy stone arches, dappled green canopy light, drifting spores, a worn path leading into shadow. Moss Sage greens and Guild Amber warmth, Slate Ink shadow depth. Horizontal parallax-friendly composition, soft depth-of-field, no characters.
backgrounds.note: WIRING GAP (review-only until wired): biome backgrounds are currently PROCEDURAL ColorRects driven by a hardcoded PALETTE in src/.../assets/screens/_shared/biome_background.gd — NO code loads a background PNG. Using real art requires swapping BiomeBackground's ColorRect for a Sprite2D/TextureRect + texture load (that file's own doc notes the swap is intended but undone). Generate to review quality/style; wiring is a follow-up.

portraits.prompt: Character roster portrait, bust framing, facing slightly off-camera: a stalwart cozy-fantasy Warrior hero — weathered iron pauldrons, a warm scarf, a kind tired face lit by lantern glow. Guild Amber and Slate Ink with Lantern Gold rim light.

enemy_sprites.prompt: 'Abyss Eel' — a pale, fast, patient eel-like dungeon enemy from a sunken-ruins biome. Side-view, full body centered, consistent silhouette, Dusk Purple and Slate Ink with a faint Lantern Gold eye-glow.
enemy_sprites.note: Switched from a 4-frame strip to a single 256x256 still: EnemySpriteFactory.get_sprite loads the sprite as ONE Texture2D with NO frame slicing (src/ui/enemy_sprite_factory.gd), so a strip renders squashed in-game. Animation needs hframes slicing added to the factory first (follow-up). The isolated/chroma-key path gives a usable still now. A strip-mode (size like 1024x256 + frames N) would need _postprocess_image to scale-with-pad instead of square-scale — also a follow-up.

class_action_sprites.prompt: OVERRIDE the single-object framing above: this asset is a 4-FRAME ACTION SPRITE SHEET, NOT one centred object and NOT an idle strip. Render FOUR figures and NO MORE — count them: 1, 2, 3, 4 — EXACTLY four full-body figures of the SAME cozy-fantasy Warrior, evenly spaced left-to-right in a single horizontal row of four equal cells, the SAME outfit, colours, scale, side-on facing and ground line throughout; ONLY the body pose advances across the cells as ONE short shield-led attack that settles back toward the neutral idle: (1) weight gathers, the round shield drawn slightly back; (2) the peak — weight drops onto the front foot and the shield leads forward in a bulwark shoulder-check, mass over flourish; (3) the shove follows through, the sword arm trailing; (4) settles back to the broad grounded stance. Controlled power, never a wild swing. The Warrior: weathered iron pauldrons, a round shield on the left arm, a warm scarf, a kind tired face, broad grounded build. Guild Amber and Slate Ink armour with a Lantern Gold rim light. Every figure is a SOLID, fully-painted body — no motion-blur, no dissolving into particles, sparks, smoke, dust or scattered dots. Clear silhouette readable at small size. Flat uniform backdrop between and around all four figures, no grid lines, no panel borders, no numbers, no arrows, no motion lines. NOT a single large figure, NOT four different characters, NEVER five or six figures.
class_action_sprites.note: PILOT COST-TEST for the Story-011 class_action_sprites bank (28 entries in full.json). This is the RISKIEST asset type: a 4-frame ACTION PROGRESSION (one motion that settles to rest), not an idle-breath strip. Prompt is byte-identical to full.json warrior_attack so the cost/quality read transfers directly. Generate this ONE first (python generate.py --manifest pilot.json --only class_action_sprites), eyeball-QA it for (a) exactly 4 figures, (b) a readable wind-up→peak→follow-through→settle progression that loops, (c) no stipple/particle dissolution, (d) palette + cozy register — THEN decide whether to scale to the full 28. Format mirrors class_sprites idle (768x432, 16:9, isolated) so ClassSpriteFactory.slice_sheet ingests it unchanged. assets/art/classes/ is TRACKED ship art (NOT gitignored — the idle sprite.png + portrait.png live committed beside this path); the generated sheet is review-only until artistic sign-off, then committed as ship art (with its .import sidecar) like the idle strips.

vfx.prompt: A single small abstract light spark for a particle texture: one warm four-point star-burst flare with a bright Lantern Gold #F2B83B hot core fading out through Guild Amber #C8872A to soft feathered edges, radially symmetric and centred. ONLY the glowing light flare itself — absolutely no chest, coin, container, lantern, creature, ground or scenery.
vfx.note: WIRING GAP (review-only until wired): no code currently loads this path. The sole VFX-texture consumer (assets/screens/dungeon_run_view/dungeon_run_view.gd, const VFX_BURST_TEXTURE_PATH) loads assets/art/demo/vfx/vfx_aura_a.png. To use this sparkle in-game, repoint that const (note: assets/art/demo/ is gitignored) or add a loader — wiring is a follow-up.
